text:
CT CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION:
48-year-old female presenting with progressive fatigue, exertional dyspnea, spontaneous gingival bleeding, night sweats, and unintentional weight loss. Laboratory workup reveals leukocytosis (WBC 87,400/uL) with circulating blasts on peripheral smear, severe anemia (hemoglobin 7.2 g/dL), and thrombocytopenia (platelets 28,000/uL). Rule out organomegaly, lymphadenopathy, and extramedullary disease. Evaluate for chloroma or myeloid sarcoma.

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following administration of intravenous contrast (iohexol 370 mg I/mL, 100 mL). Images were acquired in the portal venous phase. Axial, coronal, and sagittal multiplanar reformations were reviewed. No prior imaging available for comparison.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The lungs are clear bilaterally. No pulmonary nodules, masses, or consolidative opacities are identified. No ground-glass changes. The airways are patent to the segmental level without bronchial wall thickening or bronchiectasis. No air trapping appreciated on the available images.

Pleura: No pleural effusions, pneumothorax, or pleural thickening bilaterally.

Mediastinum and Hila: The mediastinal contour is within normal limits. The thymus demonstrates expected age-related fatty involution. No anterior, middle, or posterior mediastinal mass or adenopathy is identified. The hilar structures are symmetric without lymph node enlargement. No mediastinal widening.

Heart and Pericardium: The cardiac silhouette is normal in size and configuration. No pericardial effusion. The aortic root and thoracic aorta are within normal caliber for age. The pulmonary artery is not enlarged.

Chest Wall and Soft Tissues: No suspicious soft tissue lesion or chest wall mass. No lytic or blastic osseous lesion is appreciated in the visualized thoracic skeleton. No rib destruction.

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver is mildly enlarged, with a craniocaudal span of 17.1 cm in the right lobe on scout imaging. The hepatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal lesion, discrete mass, or perfusion abnormality. The hepatic veins and portal vein are patent with normal direction of flow on portal phase imaging. The biliary system is not dilated. No perihepatic fluid.

Spleen: Markedly enlarged, measuring 16.4 cm in the craniocaudal dimension (transverse 9.1 cm, anteroposterior 8.3 cm). The splenic parenchyma is homogeneous with uniform contrast enhancement and no focal lesion, infarct, or discrete nodular density. No perisplenic fluid or evidence of subcapsular hematoma. The splenic hilum shows no discrete nodal mass.

Pancreas: The pancreatic parenchyma is homogeneous in attenuation with normal enhancement. The pancreatic duct is not dilated. No pancreatic mass or peripancreatic lymphadenopathy.

Kidneys and Adrenal Glands: Both kidneys are normal in size and position with symmetric cortical enhancement and no focal lesion, hydronephrosis, or perinephric fluid. No adrenal mass or nodule bilaterally.

Gastrointestinal Tract: The stomach, small bowel, and visualized colon are unremarkable in wall thickness without mucosal thickening or inflammatory change. No evidence of bowel obstruction. No free intraperitoneal air. The mesentery is without discrete infiltration or soft tissue nodularity.

Lymph Nodes (Abdomen): No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes are identified in the retroperitoneum, mesentery, celiac axis, portohepatic ligament, or iliac chains. No bulky retroperitoneal adenopathy. No discrete nodal mass.

Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and inferior mesenteric artery origins appear patent. No aortic aneurysm.

Peritoneum and Ascites: No ascites. No peritoneal thickening or nodularity to suggest extramedullary deposits.

PELVIS:

Bladder: The urinary bladder is partially distended with a thin, smooth wall. No intraluminal filling defect.

Reproductive Organs: The uterus and bilateral ovaries are unremarkable in size and morphology without discrete mass or cystic change.

Pelvic Lymph Nodes: No enlarged inguinal, external iliac, or obturator lymph nodes. No discrete pelvic soft tissue mass.

Musculoskeletal (Pelvis and Visualized Lower Spine): The osseous structures of the pelvis, sacrum, and visualized lumbar spine show no focal lytic or sclerotic lesion. No evidence of osseous erosion or pathologic fracture. No periosteal reaction.

Soft Tissue Masses: No discrete soft tissue mass or tumor deposit is identified in the pelvis or perineum to suggest a chloroma or myeloid sarcoma.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Marked splenomegaly measuring 16.4 cm craniocaudal with homogeneous parenchyma, in the setting of the provided clinical history of a suspected hematologic malignancy with circulating blasts. No splenic lesion, infarct, or perforated capsule identified.

2. Mild hepatomegaly with a right lobe craniocaudal span of 17.1 cm. The hepatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal lesion; findings are consistent with secondary involvement in the setting of a hematologic process rather than primary hepatic pathology.

3. No lymphadenopathy above or below the diaphragm. No mediastinal, hilar, retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic adenopathy.

4. No evidence of discrete extramedullary leukemic involvement. No soft tissue mass, nodule, or enhancing deposit in any organ, soft tissue compartment, or osseous structure to suggest a chloroma or myeloid sarcoma. No peritoneal or pleural disease.

5. Clear lungs bilaterally. No pulmonary infiltrate, consolidation, or pulmonary mass. No pleural or pericardial effusion.

6. Remaining solid abdominal and pelvic organs are unremarkable.

Overall, the imaging findings are consistent with an underlying hematologic malignancy, with organomegaly (splenomegaly and mild hepatomegaly) as the predominant findings. No solid organ metastatic deposits or extramedullary disease are demonstrated.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Body Imaging Division]
Technologist: [CT Technologist]
Study Status: Final
